oligos.name: Round1 barcoded RT primer, anchored poly(dT)15 (Round1_01–48)
oligos.role: Round-1 barcoded reverse-transcription primer (anchored oligo-dT); appends cell barcode 1
oligos.notes: 5' phosphate (/5Phos/). AGGCCAGAGCATTCG is the round-1 constant/linker region; next 8 nt = well-specific round-1 barcode; TTTTTTTTTTTTTTT = dT15; VN anchor. Example Round1_01 barcode = AACGTGAT. 48 dT primers (wells A1–D12), one per well; combined with the matching random-hexamer primer for RT.

oligos.name: Round1 barcoded RT primer, random hexamer (Round1_49–96)
oligos.role: Round-1 barcoded reverse-transcription primer (random hexamer); appends cell barcode 1
oligos.notes: 5' phosphate (/5Phos/). Same AGGCCAGAGCATTCG constant + 8-nt round-1 barcode, ending in a random hexamer (NNNNNN). Example Round1_49 barcode = GATAGACA. Random hexamer and dT15 primers sharing the same barcode are pooled in each RT well (e.g. E1 mixed with A1).

oligos.name: Round2 barcode strand (Round2_01–96)
oligos.role: Round-2 ligation barcode strand; appends cell barcode 2 to the 5' end of the RT primer
oligos.notes: 5' phosphate (/5Phos/). CATCGGCGTACGACT and ATCCACGTGCTTGAG are constant ligation-junction regions; central 8 nt = round-2 barcode. Example Round2_01 barcode = AACGTGAT. Annealed to linker BC_0215 (11 µM) with 12 µM barcode before in-cell ligation.

oligos.name: Round3 barcode strand (Round3_01–96)
oligos.role: Round-3 ligation barcode strand; appends UMI + cell barcode 3 + PCR handle + biotin
oligos.notes: 5' biotin (/5Biosg/). CAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT = universal PCR/Read-2 handle; NNNNNNNNNN = 10-nt UMI; next 8 nt = round-3 barcode; GTGGCCGATGTTTCG = constant ligation-junction region. Example Round3_01 barcode = AACGTGAT. Annealed to linker BC_0060 (13 µM) with 14 µM barcode.

oligos.name: BC_0215 – Round 2 barcode linker
oligos.role: Bridge/linker oligo that hybridizes across the round-1/round-2 ligation junction (not incorporated into product)
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of blocking strand BC_0216; scaffolds ligation of round-2 barcode onto the RT primer.

oligos.name: BC_0216 – Round 2 blocking strand
oligos.role: Blocking strand complementary to the 3' overhang of round-2 barcodes; prevents mislabeling
oligos.notes: Used at 26.4 µM in 2.5X T4 ligase buffer after round-2 ligation.

oligos.name: BC_0060 – Round 3 barcode linker
oligos.role: Bridge/linker oligo that hybridizes across the round-2/round-3 ligation junction (not incorporated)
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of blocking strand BC_0066.

oligos.name: BC_0066 – Round 3 blocking strand
oligos.role: Blocking strand complementary to the 3' overhang of round-3 barcodes; prevents mislabeling
oligos.notes: Used at 30.8 µM in 150 mM EDTA (terminates ligase activity) after round-3 ligation.

oligos.name: BC_0127 – Template switching primer (TSO)
oligos.role: Template-switch oligo; adds a universal handle to the 3' end of first-strand cDNA
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified, from Exiqon. 3' end is rG rG +G (two ribo-G followed by a locked-nucleic-acid G) — folded here to GGG. Used at 100 µM in the on-bead Maxima H Minus template-switch reaction.

oligos.name: BC_0108 – PCR primer (TSO handle)
oligos.role: cDNA-amplification PCR primer binding the template-switch handle
oligos.notes: Used with BC_0062 in the 5-cycle + qPCR amplification after template switching.

oligos.name: BC_0062 – PCR primer (Round-3 handle)
oligos.role: cDNA-amplification PCR primer binding the round-3 universal PCR handle
oligos.notes: Used with BC_0108. Same sequence serves as the Read-2 handle in the final library.

oligos.name: BC_0118 – Nextera P5 primer (N501)
oligos.role: P5/i5 library PCR primer added during indexing PCR after tagmentation
oligos.notes: Binds the Nextera adapter on the tagmented (cDNA) end; used with one indexed P7 primer (BC_0076–BC_0083).

oligos.name: BC_0076 – Nextera indexed P7 primer (TSBC07, sublibrary index #1)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 library PCR primer; binds the round-3 handle and adds the sublibrary (4th) barcode

components.name: sublibrary index
components.role: 6-nt i7 sublibrary index (read as the 6-nt index read)

components.name: handle
components.role: priming region binding round-3 PCR handle

oligos.notes: Used with P5 primer BC_0118. The 6-nt index is the fourth barcode read in the 6-cycle index read.

oligos.name: BC_0077 – Nextera indexed P7 primer (TSBC08, sublibrary index #2)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 library PCR primer

components.name: handle
components.role: priming region binding round-3 PCR handle

oligos.notes: Used with P5 primer BC_0118.

oligos.name: BC_0078 – Nextera indexed P7 primer (TSBC09, sublibrary index #3)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 library PCR primer

components.name: handle
components.role: priming region binding round-3 PCR handle

oligos.notes: Used with P5 primer BC_0118.

oligos.name: BC_0079 – Nextera indexed P7 primer (TSBC10, sublibrary index #4)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 library PCR primer

components.name: handle
components.role: priming region binding round-3 PCR handle

oligos.notes: Used with P5 primer BC_0118.

oligos.name: BC_0080 – Nextera indexed P7 primer (TSBC11, sublibrary index #5)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 library PCR primer

components.name: handle
components.role: priming region binding round-3 PCR handle

oligos.notes: Used with P5 primer BC_0118.

oligos.name: BC_0081 – Nextera indexed P7 primer (TSBC12, sublibrary index #6)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 library PCR primer

components.name: handle
components.role: priming region binding round-3 PCR handle

oligos.notes: Used with P5 primer BC_0118.

oligos.name: BC_0082 – Nextera indexed P7 primer (TSBC13, sublibrary index #7)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 library PCR primer

components.name: handle
components.role: priming region binding round-3 PCR handle

oligos.notes: Used with P5 primer BC_0118.

oligos.name: BC_0083 – Nextera indexed P7 primer (TSBC14, sublibrary index #8)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 library PCR primer

components.name: handle
components.role: priming region binding round-3 PCR handle

oligos.notes: Used with P5 primer BC_0118.

final_library.source_label: SPLiT-seq final Illumina library (after tagmentation + indexing PCR)

strands.direction: bottom (5'->3', P7 to P5 / cDNA-barcode strand)

annotation_lines: i5 fixed index TAGATCGC (N501) + S5 (TCGTCGGCAGCGTC) — Read-1 priming region
ME (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG) — Nextera mosaic end added by tagmentation
A15 + anchor (revcomp of dT15VN of the RT primer)
BC1 (8 nt, round-1 cell barcode) — appended during RT
round-1 linker constant (revcomp of AGGCCAGAGCATTCG + ATCCACGTGCTTGAG)
BC2 (8 nt, round-2 cell barcode) — appended by ligation
round-2/3 linker constant (revcomp of GTGGCCGATGTTTCG + CATCGGCGTACGACT)
BC3 (8 nt, round-3 cell barcode) — appended by ligation
UMI (10 nt) — part of the round-3 oligo
Read-2 handle AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTG (revcomp of CAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT) — Read-2 priming region
SAMPLE_INDEX (6 nt sublibrary/4th barcode) — index read

evidence.locator: SPLiT-seq final Illumina library (after tagmentation + indexing PCR)

library_generation.title: In-cell barcoded reverse transcription (barcode 1)
library_generation.summary: Fixed cells are split into up to 48 wells and in-cell RT with well-specific barcoded dT15/hexamer primers converts RNA to cDNA while appending cell barcode 1.
library_generation.note: Maxima H Minus RT in permeabilized formaldehyde-fixed cells; each well gets a pooled dT15VN + random-hexamer primer sharing the same 8-nt round-1 barcode. Cells are then pooled.

library_generation.title: Split-pool ligation round 2 (barcode 2)
library_generation.summary: Pooled cells are re-split into a 96-well plate and an in-cell T4 ligation appends cell barcode 2 to the 5' end of the RT primer.
library_generation.note: Round-2 barcode strand is pre-annealed to linker BC_0215, which bridges the ligation junction; blocking strand BC_0216 then quenches unused barcodes before pooling.

library_generation.title: Split-pool ligation round 3 (barcode 3 + UMI + handle + biotin)
library_generation.summary: A third split-pool ligation appends cell barcode 3 together with a 10-nt UMI, a universal PCR handle and a 5' biotin.
library_generation.note: Round-3 barcode strand pre-annealed to linker BC_0060; blocking strand BC_0066 in EDTA terminates ligation. The 5' biotin enables downstream streptavidin capture.

library_generation.title: Lysis, sublibrary split, streptavidin capture
library_generation.summary: Cells are pooled, aliquoted into sublibraries, lysed to reverse crosslinks, and the biotinylated barcoded cDNA is captured on streptavidin beads.
library_generation.note: Proteinase K + heat reverse formaldehyde crosslinks; Dynabeads MyOne Streptavidin C1 bind the 5'-biotin. The number of sublibraries sets how many 4th-barcode PCRs follow.

library_generation.title: Template switching
library_generation.summary: On-bead Maxima RT template-switches at the cDNA 3' end, adding a universal TSO handle.
library_generation.note: TSO BC_0127 (…GAATGGG, rGrG+G) adds its complement to the cDNA, giving a second universal handle for PCR.

library_generation.title: PCR amplification of barcoded cDNA
library_generation.summary: PCR with BC_0062 (round-3 handle) and BC_0108 (TSO handle) amplifies the full-length barcoded cDNA.
library_generation.note: 5 cycles of Kapa HiFi followed by qPCR to endpoint (EvaGreen), then SPRI cleanup.

library_generation.title: Nextera tagmentation
library_generation.summary: Nextera XT transposase fragments the amplified cDNA and inserts mosaic-end adapters, retaining fragments that carry the barcode/UMI end.
library_generation.note: 600 pg cDNA tagmented; only fragments spanning the round-3 barcode handle and a new ME end are usable for the indexing PCR.

library_generation.title: Indexing PCR (barcode 4) → final library
library_generation.summary: PCR with P5 primer BC_0118 (binds the ME/S5 end) and one indexed P7 primer BC_0076–BC_0083 (binds the round-3 handle) adds P5/i5 and P7/sublibrary index to yield the Illumina library.
library_generation.note: 12 cycles; each sublibrary gets a distinct 6-nt i7 index (the 4th barcode). 0.7X SPRI cleanup.

library_sequencing.note: 66-nt cDNA read from the tagmented end. The Nextera Read-1 primer anneals across the S5/mosaic-end region on the bottom strand and reads into the transcript fragment.

94-nt read = UMI(10) + BC3(8) + round3/2 linker(30) + BC2(8) + round2/1 linker(30) + BC1(8). Reads with >1 mismatch in any of the three 8-nt cell barcodes, or >1 low-quality UMI base (phred<=10), are discarded.
6-nt index read = the fourth (sublibrary) barcode introduced during the indexing PCR after tagmentation. The i7 index-read primer anneals to the top strand and reads across the 6-nt index.
library_sequencing.diagram:
GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT------->
5'-...GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT[i7:6]...-3'   (i7 index-read primer, extends into index)
3'-...CACTGACCTCAAGTCTGCACACGAGAAGGCTAGA[i7:6]...-5'   (top strand, template)

title: SPLiT-seq
description: SPLiT-seq (split-pool ligation-based transcriptome sequencing) is a combinatorial-barcoding single-cell / single-nucleus RNA-seq method that labels each transcriptome's cellular origin without ever physically isolating single cells — the fixed cells or nuclei themselves act as reaction compartments. Formaldehyde-fixed cells pass through four rounds of barcoding: an in-cell barcoded reverse-transcription round (barcode 1), two in-cell split-pool ligation rounds (barcodes 2 and 3, with the round-3 oligo also adding a UMI, a PCR handle and a biotin), and a final sublibrary-splitting PCR (barcode 4). Each cell thus receives a unique 4-barcode combination. After streptavidin capture, template switching, PCR and Nextera tagmentation, Read 1 sequences the cDNA fragment, Read 2 reads the UMI plus the three ligated cell barcodes, and the index read reads the sublibrary barcode.

original_publication.title: Single-cell profiling of the developing mouse brain and spinal cord with split-pool barcoding

throughput.summary: 156,049 single-nucleus transcriptomes profiled in one experiment; four rounds of barcoding give 21,233,664 combinations (3×96-well + 24 PCR), enough to uniquely label >1 million cells, at ~$0.01–0.02 library-prep cost per cell.
throughput.cells: 156,049 single nuclei analyzed (P2/P11 mouse brain + spinal cord); scalable to hundreds of thousands / >1,000,000
throughput.rna: Deep sublibrary (131 nuclei): median 4,943 UMIs / 2,055 genes per nucleus; species-mixing whole cells: median 15,365 UMIs / 5,498 genes per human cell
throughput.dna: N/A (poly(A) mRNA / cDNA assay; no genomic DNA readout)

other.label: Fixation compatibility
other.value: Works with formaldehyde-fixed cells or nuclei; no custom equipment (pipetting-only workflow)

other.label: Barcode collisions
other.value: ~2.5% expected collisions at 150,000 nuclei; species-mixing purity >99%

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed by their four combinatorial barcodes and quality-filtered, the cDNA read (Read 1) is aligned with STAR and assigned to genes with the Drop-seq tools, UMIs are collapsed with Starcode to build a cell-by-gene count matrix, which is then iteratively clustered (high-variance gene selection + PCA + Jaccard-Louvain), visualized with Barnes-Hut t-SNE, and ordered into developmental lineages/pseudotime.
data_processing.statistical_model: Loess dispersion fit for high-variance gene selection; k=15 kNN Jaccard-Louvain modularity clustering; DBSCAN + Monocle 2 pseudotime ordering.
